description:
🕒 17:30–20:30

Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Hanno Ecotourism Association: https://hanno-tourism.jp/hanno-eco/tour/ec-morimana-satoyamahotaru/

This firefly observation takes place at "Aoji-shi Valley" (アオジシ谷), where environmental conservation work protects fireflies. It's an evening-to-night tour where you'll learn thoroughly about Genji fireflies and Heike fireflies—their bioluminescence secrets, ecology, history, culture, and surprising connections to people. You'll go deep into what most people don't know about these creatures.

【Fee】
Adults: ¥6,000
Students: ¥5,000
(includes guide, insurance, materials)

【Capacity】15 people

【Guide】Mori no Field School staff

【What to bring】Drinks, flashlight, light snacks, change of shoes

【More information】Mori no Field School website: https://morinomanabiya168.wixsite.com/website
location: 集合 飯能市立博物館前（国際興業バス「OH!!!・天覧山下」または「市民会館・博物館」下車徒歩3分)
summary: What is the light that floats in early summer? ~ Firefly observation in the countryside at night ~
